meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve can't validate ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't validate ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Validate Ideas (Case Study)
content.seo_description: Stop guessing if your app will sell. Use this beginner-friendly guide to set up high-impact analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate ideas fast.
content.answer_block: Stop building in the dark. By integrating event-based analytics into your MVP, you transform subjective guesses into objective data. Use Antigravity to scaffold your telemetry and Claude Code to inject tracking logic, ensuring you capture user intent from the first click, ultimately validating your idea through real-world usage data.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect UI, shipping features that look stunning in your portfolio. Yet, the silence from your users is deafening. The nightmare of the freelance developer isn't bad code—it's building a product nobody actually wants. You are flying blind, relying on intuition while your billable hours evaporate.

But what if you could turn on the lights? By treating analytics as a first-class citizen of your development stack, you shift from 'guessing' to 'guiding.' This case study demonstrates how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build a high-fidelity feedback loop that proves your idea's value before you write your next line of feature code.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before touching code, define the single action that proves user intent. Is it a sign-up? A button click? A file upload?
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a lightweight analytics event tracker focused on tracking user intent events like 'signup_initiated' and 'feature_clicked'. Include properties for timestamp, user_id, and session_metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Review my current project structure and propose a clean directory for an analytics-service.ts file.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Telemetry Layer
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a robust service that handles event queuing and batching, preventing performance degradation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a TypeScript analytics service that batches events and sends them to a mock endpoint, ensuring asynchronous execution so the UI remains performant.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Generate a reusable analytics hook that can be imported into any React component.'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Identify the high-friction points in your app and inject tracking code to capture drop-off rates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a wrapper function for button clicks that automatically logs the element ID and the current page path to our analytics service.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Inject the tracking hook into the primary call-to-action buttons across the landing page.'

steps.title: Implement Session Persistence
steps.description: Ensure you aren't counting anonymous users as unique visitors. Track unique sessions to get accurate conversion data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a utility to generate and store a persistent user_id in localStorage, ensuring it persists across page refreshes for unified session tracking.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'How can I ensure the user_id utility handles cross-tab sessions effectively?'

steps.title: Visualize and Iterate
steps.description: Connect your event stream to a dashboard to see, in real-time, how users interact with your MVP.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a summary report template that parses our event logs and calculates daily active users (DAU) and conversion rates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a simple admin view that displays the event log stream for debugging purposes.'

pro_tips: Track 'intent' events (clicks on disabled features) to discover what users want before you build it.
Always set up a 'heartbeat' event to measure how long users keep your application tab open.
Use server-side logging for sensitive data to avoid exposing API keys in your client-side bundle.
Set up automated alerts for when a critical funnel step conversion rate drops below 10%.
Keep your analytics payload under 1KB to ensure zero impact on your core web vitals.
content.next_steps: Deploy your telemetry-enabled MVP to a staging environment, invite 10 users to test it, and review the event logs to identify the biggest drop-off point in your user journey.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to stop guessing and start scaling based on hard, undeniable data. Don't let your next project fail in silence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
